# OpenTelemetry PyMySQL Instrumentation

{{ pypi }}

## Installation

```
pip install opentelemetry-instrumentation-pymysql
```

## References

- [OpenTelemetry PyMySQL Instrumentation](https://opentelemetry-python-contrib.readthedocs.io/en/latest/instrumentation/pymysql/pymysql.html)

modules.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql
documentation:
The integration with PyMySQL supports the [PyMySQL] library and can be enabled
by using `PyMySQLInstrumentor`.

# Usage

```python
import pymysql
from opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql import PyMySQLInstrumentor


PyMySQLInstrumentor().instrument()

cnx = pymysql.connect(database="MySQL_Database")
cursor = cnx.cursor()
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO test (testField) VALUES (123)"
cnx.commit()
cursor.close()
cnx.close()
```

# API

[pymysql]: https://pypi.org/project/PyMySQL/

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql.PyMySQLInstrumentor._instrument
documentation:
Integrate with the PyMySQL library.
https://github.com/PyMySQL/PyMySQL/

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql.PyMySQLInstrumentor._uninstrument
documentation: "Disable PyMySQL instrumentation

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql.PyMySQLInstrumentor.instrument_connection
documentation:
Enable instrumentation in a PyMySQL connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to instrument.
    tracer_provider: The optional tracer provider to use. If omitted
        the current globally configured one is used.

Returns:
    An instrumented connection.

methods.name: opentelemetry.instrumentation.pymysql.PyMySQLInstrumentor.uninstrument_connection
documentation:
Disable instrumentation in a PyMySQL connection.

Args:
    connection: The connection to uninstrument.

Returns:
    An uninstrumented connection.
